title: acer negundo f. variegatum
intro: Acer negundo f. variegatum, also known as the variegated boxelder, is a plant species that belongs to the family Sapindaceae and is native to North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Acer negundo f. variegatum include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and aiding in the treatment of various skin issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves, bark, or roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
